ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee class (FOODON_03301075). No specific Starbucks VIA Colombia entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers instant coffee generically. No brand-specific code exists for Starbucks VIA Colombia.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 is the closest parent substance concept for coffee. No instant-coffee-specific or brand-specific SNOMED code exists.

serving.description: 1 stick (approximately 3.3 g dissolved in 237 mL hot water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one VIA stick packet (approximately 3.3 g of instant coffee powder) dissolved in 237 mL (8 fl oz) of hot water, yielding a brewed cup of approximately 240 mL.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks VIA Instant Coffee Colombia — USDA Branded Food (FDC ID 2103705)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA Branded Food entry for Starbucks VIA Instant Coffee Colombia provides per-serving and per-100g nutrient data. Values below are expressed per 100 g of dry instant coffee powder. One stick is ~3.3 g; per-100g values scaled accordingly. Energy per 100g dry powder is approximately 364 kcal based on macronutrient composition (carbohydrate ~64g, protein ~14g, fat ~0g). Cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entry for instant coffee (FDC ID 171891) for secondary confirmation.

gi_source.title: Black coffee and instant coffee — glycemic index reference; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain black coffee and instant coffee have a negligible glycemic response due to the absence of digestible carbohydrates in the prepared beverage. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) confirm that black coffee has an effectively zero glycemic index. Per the zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive value) and gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because the prepared beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Starbucks VIA Colombia instant coffee is very low in sodium (~1 mg per prepared cup) and does not contribute meaningfully to dietary sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware that caffeine (~95–130 mg per serving) may cause a transient, short-lived rise in blood pressure, and habitual moderate coffee consumption has not been shown to increase long-term hypertension risk in most adults.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic impact; no added sugar in plain preparation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain Starbucks VIA Colombia instant coffee prepared without added sugar or milk has a negligible glycemic index (effectively zero) and does not raise postprandial blood glucose; epidemiological evidence suggests habitual unsweetened coffee consumption may be associated with reduced risk of Type 2 Diabetes, and this product is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without caloric additives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat; contains chlorogenic acids and antioxidants
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Starbucks VIA Colombia instant coffee contains negligible saturated fat and no cholesterol in the prepared beverage; the arabica beans provide chlorogenic acids and antioxidants that have been associated with modest cardioprotective effects in observational studies, and moderate unsweetened coffee consumption is generally considered compatible with a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks VIA Instant Coffee Colombia is a single-serve instant coffee product made from 100% Colombian arabica beans using Starbucks' proprietary microground coffee technology, which combines finely ground roasted coffee with soluble instant coffee to deliver a flavour profile closer to brewed coffee than conventional instant coffee. Each box contains 12 individual stick packets, each containing approximately 3.3 g of dry coffee powder intended to be dissolved in 237 mL (8 fl oz) of hot water. The flavour profile is described as balanced and nutty, characteristic of Colombian arabica beans grown at high altitude, which typically exhibit mild acidity, medium body, and notes of caramel and walnut. As a plain black instant coffee, the prepared beverage is essentially calorie-free per cup (approximately 5–10 kcal per serving), contains no fat, no sugar, and negligible sodium, making it suitable for calorie-conscious individuals, people with Type 2 Diabetes, and those following low-sodium or low-fat dietary patterns. The product is sold in Singapore via field survey at Starbucks outlets and is positioned as a premium at-home or on-the-go coffee solution. Clinically, the primary consideration is caffeine content (approximately 95–130 mg per serving), which may affect individuals sensitive to stimulants or those with certain cardiovascular conditions. The product contains no dairy, no gluten, and no added sugar in its base formulation.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Starbucks VIA Colombia?
Is Starbucks VIA Colombia suitable for diabetics?
Does Starbucks VIA Colombia contain sugar?
How much caffeine is in Starbucks VIA Colombia?
Is Starbucks VIA Colombia gluten free?
What is the GI of instant coffee?
How to prepare Starbucks VIA instant coffee

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA Branded Foods (FDC) for Starbucks VIA Instant Coffee Colombia, which is an approved secondary global source for packaged commercial products. The match is exact at the brand and product level. GI is an analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed literature for plain black coffee, as no direct GI study exists for this specific branded instant coffee product. Confidence is medium rather than high because the per-100g values represent dry powder composition, and the prepared beverage nutrient profile differs substantially; the primary clinical use case is the prepared beverage.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are expressed per 100 g of dry instant coffee powder as sold. The prepared beverage (1 stick in 237 mL water) contains approximately 5 kcal, 0.5 g protein, 0 g fat, and 2.1 g carbohydrate per cup. 2. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates in prepared form; gi_category is 'low' per convention for zero-GI beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. 3. sugar_g is 0.0 per USDA source — confirmed zero by product formulation (no added sugar). 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 per USDA source — confirmed zero for instant coffee powder. 5. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source. 7. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; coffee contains no cholesterol. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 9. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 10. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product. 11. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 12. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 13. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 14. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 15. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 16. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 17. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 18. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 19. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source for this product. 20. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 21. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 22. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 23. This is a Coffee-cuisine product sold globally; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply. local_names fields are null as this is a branded Western commercial product with no local-language canonical names. 24. field survey data lists this product at SGD 16.70 for a 12-stick box at Starbucks One Fullerton.
